key_risks: Rapid Strait of Hormuz de-escalation could collapse oil prices and energy sector gains
Hawkish Warsh Fed surprise at June 17 FOMC could pressure gold and equities simultaneously
Semiconductor momentum reversal if hyperscaler capex guidance disappoints in earnings
Mega-cap tech narrow leadership continuing to dominate SPY benchmark, creating alpha drag
Consumer staples underperformance if risk-on rally resumes on cooling inflation print

portfolio.rationale: Strait of Hormuz disruption with WTI at $105 and Brent $109; energy sector showing strongest momentum (+8% 30d) with clear supply-side catalyst persisting through window.
Inflation re-acceleration (CPI 3.8% YoY), geopolitical tension, weak consumer sentiment (48.2), and Fed leadership uncertainty support gold despite recent pullback; counter-trend entry after -6% 30d.
With 30Y at 5.11%, hike probability >30%, and inflation reaccelerating, short duration preserves capital while yielding ~4%+; avoids duration risk into June FOMC.
Hyperscaler capex >$600B 2026-27 and $1.29T semi market forecast; strong momentum (+20% 30d) backed by structural AI demand despite stretched valuations.
Defensive ballast given consumer sentiment collapse, sticky inflation, and high gasoline prices pressuring discretionary spending; positive recent performance amid market stress.

portfolio_rationale: Stagflation-tilted barbell: energy and gold capture the inflation/geopolitical impulse from Hormuz disruption and resurgent CPI, short-duration Treasuries hedge against further rate hike risk under incoming Chair Warsh, semis provide structural AI growth exposure, and staples add defensive ballast given collapsing consumer sentiment (48.2).
rationale_summary: Positioning for stagflationary conditions with energy/gold inflation hedges, short-duration cash equivalent against rate-hike risk, selective AI growth via semis, and defensive staples — avoiding rate-sensitive long duration and weakening cyclicals.
